title: Power Commands — investigate, compare, chain
summary: Session 25 adds three power commands that push every layer of the Octane stack to maximum depth. `octane investigate` decomposes a query into parallel research dimensions, runs each through the full web + memory agent stack, then synthesises a structured multi-section report. `octane compare` builds an N×M research matrix (items × dimensions), fills every cell in parallel via WebAgent, then produces a side-by-side comparison with a verdict. `octane chain` executes a user-defined multi-step pipeline with {ref} interpolation between steps — the foundation for saved, reusable workflows.

files_created.description: DimensionPlanner — decomposes a query into 2-8 independent research dimensions via REASON tier LLM. Falls back to keyword-derived dimensions (finance/tech/generic) if Bodega is unavailable. Outputs ResearchDimension + DimensionPlan dataclasses.

ComparisonPlanner — identifies comparison items and dimensions for `octane compare`. Includes extract_items_from_query() for parsing 'X vs Y', 'X versus Y', 'compare X and Y' patterns. Produces a task_matrix of all (item × dimension) pairs for parallel research.

ChainParser — purely deterministic parser for `octane chain` step strings. No LLM, no I/O. Parses 'name: command args {ref}' format, validates {ref} references (no forward refs), extracts {{template_vars}}, and produces an ordered ChainPlan.

InvestigateOrchestrator — the engine behind `octane investigate`. Parallel research via asyncio.Semaphore(4), REASON tier synthesis, streaming events: plan → finding → synthesis → done. Graceful degradation when web agent unavailable.

CompareOrchestrator — the engine behind `octane compare`. N×M parallel cell research via asyncio.Semaphore(4), REASON tier matrix synthesis, streaming events: plan → cell → matrix → synthesis → done. Structured output: dimension sections + markdown table + verdict.

ChainExecutor — executes a parsed ChainPlan step by step. Dispatches each step to the right backend (ask → Orchestrator/Bodega, search/fetch → WebAgent, synthesize → REASON tier, code → stub). Stores step outputs for {ref} interpolation. --save serialises the chain to a reusable JSON workflow file.
81-test suite covering all Session 25 components. Fully mocked — no network, no Bodega, no Redis.

files_modified.description: Added three new top-level commands: investigate, compare, chain. Each uses Rich Live progress display (spinner + bar + per-item status). investigate streams finding-by-finding; compare streams cell-by-cell; chain streams step-by-step. All three fall back gracefully on errors.

new_commands.name: investigate
new_commands.help: 🔍 Investigate a topic across multiple independent research dimensions

new_commands.name: compare
new_commands.help: ⚖️  Compare items across multiple dimensions in a structured matrix

new_commands.name: chain
new_commands.help: ⛓️  Execute a multi-step pipeline with variable interpolation

files_modified.change: Fixed extract_items_from_query() to return [] instead of None when no comparison pattern is detected (was breaking isinstance checks in callers).

test_results.full_suite: 711 passed, 8 failed (pre-existing matplotlib failures — unchanged from Session 24)

architecture_decisions.decision: Streaming-first design for all three orchestrators
architecture_decisions.rationale: All heavy operations (parallel dimension research, matrix cell research, chain steps) can take 10-60s. Users need progressive feedback. run_stream() yields typed events; run() wraps it for non-streaming callers.
Bodega runs on local Apple Silicon with limited RAM. 4 concurrent LLM calls is safe for an 8B model; more would cause memory pressure and latency spikes.
Chain step parsing must be instantaneous, predictable, and offline-capable. LLM parsing would add latency and nondeterminism to step wiring. All intelligence in the chain happens at execution time.

All three commands work with partial or zero data. If web agent is down, findings/cells are empty but synthesis still runs. If Bodega is down, planners fall back to keyword heuristics. octane never crashes on infra issues.

architecture_decisions.decision: DimensionPlanner and ComparisonPlanner use REASON tier at temperature=0
architecture_decisions.rationale: Planning is a single, deterministic cognitive task. The quality of the plan determines the quality of all downstream research. REASON is the highest-quality local tier. Temperature=0 ensures repeatable decomposition.

architecture_decisions.decision: --save for chain produces a reusable workflow JSON
architecture_decisions.rationale: Power users will want to define once, run many times — e.g. a daily earnings briefing chain. The JSON format (type: octane_chain, steps: [{name, command, args}]) is the foundation for Session 26's workflow scheduler.

cli_usage_examples: octane investigate 'Is NVDA overvalued at current levels?'
octane compare 'NVDA vs AMD vs INTC'
octane compare 'compare React, Vue, and Svelte for a startup'
octane chain 'ask What are NVDA main products' 'synthesize {prev}'
octane chain 'prices: fetch NVDA' 'report: synthesize {prices}' --save daily_brief.json
next_session_seeds: Session 26: Workflow Scheduler — load + run saved chain JSON files on a schedule (cron-style), persist results to memory, surface via `octane workflow list/run/schedule`
Session 26: investigate --deep flag — second-pass follow-up queries per dimension using {finding} refs
